title_en: She Fought the Far Right Online for Years. Now She Wants to Do It in Congress
title_zh: 她在网上与极右翼斗争了多年。如今她想把这场斗争带进国会

paragraphs.en: Kat Abughazaleh knows how to create viral moments online. She’s an old pro, in fact, capable of posting clips of herself that can rack up millions of views. But engineering attention in the physical world is a decidedly different challenge—one that Abughazaleh was struggling to solve on an overcast afternoon in July.
paragraphs.zh: 卡特·阿布加扎勒（Kat Abughazaleh）知道如何在网络上制造爆点。她其实是个“老手”，能发布自己的视频片段并吸引上百万次观看。但在现实世界里争取关注完全是另一种挑战——也是阿布加扎勒在七月一个阴天午后努力想要解决的问题。

paragraphs.en: For our politics issue, WIRED examines the state of tech’s influence on governmental power—and the people who will change everything in the future.
paragraphs.zh: 在本期政治专刊中，《WIRED》将审视科技对政府权力影响的现状，以及未来将改变一切的人。

paragraphs.en: The 26-year-old, who is running for Congress in Illinois, was sitting outside her campaign headquarters in Chicago, trying to connect with prospective voters during the area’s annual taco crawl. Some people were stopping to chat, but others hurried along in search of their next carnitas fix. On the internet, Abughazaleh can precision-edit a video to maximize eyeballs. On Clark Street, a busy thoroughfare that cuts across the city’s northeastern neighborhoods, she had to convince people cruising for excellent Mexican food that she was worth delaying their next bite.
paragraphs.zh: 这位正在伊利诺伊州竞选国会议员的26岁候选人坐在芝加哥的竞选总部外，试图在当地一年一度的塔可“巡游”活动中与潜在选民接触。有人停下来聊天，但也有人匆匆赶路，去寻找下一份炖烤猪肉卷（carnitas）。在网上，阿布加扎勒可以精准剪辑一段视频来最大化吸引目光。在芝加哥东北社区间贯通市区的繁忙大道克拉克街（Clark Street）上，她则得说服正在寻找优质墨西哥菜的路人，认为值得让她打断他们下一口美食。

paragraphs.en: That day, at least, the solution was simple: hot sauce. Abughazaleh got a taste for the stuff growing up in Texas and never let go. She hauled out the collection of bottles she keeps in her office—many of them sent by a supporter—and lined up over a dozen on a table for pedestrians to douse their tacos. Within minutes, locals sloshing to-go margaritas in plastic cups stopped to pepper her with questions, like her stance on organized labor (pro), what her “main issue” is (“antiauthoritarianism”), and her thoughts on contemporary sci-fi and fantasy (Red Rising is superior to A Song of Ice and Fire). It seemed every other person had a friend who was a big fan. Supporters in cars honked periodically. With help from a volunteer who translated, Abughazaleh explained to a Spanish-speaking family that her office doubles as a mutual aid hub, stocked with free pantry items and open to “everyone except ICE.” They took campaign stickers.
paragraphs.zh: 那天，至少解决办法很简单：辣酱。阿布加扎勒在德州长大时就迷上了这种东西，而且一直没放弃。她拿出办公室里收藏的一排辣酱瓶——很多是支持者送来的——在桌上排了十几瓶，供过往行人给他们的塔可浇上。几分钟内，手里拎着塑料杯外带玛格丽特的当地人就停下来向她连珠炮似地提问：她对工会（支持）怎么看，她的“最主要议题”是什么（“反权威主义”），以及她对当代科幻和奇幻作品的看法（《红色崛起》比《冰与火之歌》更好）。看起来几乎每隔一位旁观者就有一个朋友是铁杆粉丝。车里等候的支持者时不时按喇叭鸣笛。在一位会翻译的志愿者帮忙下，阿布加扎勒向一个西语家庭解释，她的办公室兼作互助中心，里边备有免费食品储备，向“除ICE外的所有人”开放。他们接受了竞选贴纸。

paragraphs.en: An older man with white whiskers and a “Make America Green Again” hat trotted over to the table after locking up his bike. Abughazaleh leaned forward, eager to engage on the issues. Before she got going, though, the man cut her off. “You have terrible posture,” he chided her, explaining that he was a physical therapist. “Pull up your breastbone. Shoulders back.”
paragraphs.zh: 一位留着白胡子的中年男子把自行车上锁后，戴着“让美国再次变绿”帽子快步走到桌前。阿布加扎勒（Abughazaleh）身体前倾，显得很想参与讨论。就在她开口之前，这位男士先打断了她。“你姿势很差，”他说着自我介绍自己是物理治疗师，“把胸骨挺起来，肩膀往后放。”

paragraphs.en: Abughazaleh mimicked the man’s movements, squaring her shoulders in her loose jean jacket and doing a heroic job at not looking annoyed. She asked what issues he cares about as he inspected her bearing. Turns out, they had plenty to discuss: ICE raids, the Supreme Court’s perfidy. By the end of the conversation, the man was grabbing pamphlets and didn’t seem to notice that Abughazaleh had curled herself back into a more comfortable position.
paragraphs.zh: 阿布加扎勒照着那位男子的动作做了模仿，在宽松牛仔夹克里挺直肩膀，尽量不让自己露出恼怒的表情，还问他最关心哪些议题，因为他正在检查她的站姿。结果他们有很多话题可聊：ICE 的突击抓捕、最高法院的背信行为。谈到最后，这位男子正抓起小册子，似乎都没注意到阿布加扎勒已经舒服地缩回身形。

paragraphs.en: Seeing her deal with even the most condescending of constituents, you wouldn’t know that this is Abughazaleh’s first time running for office. She is at the forefront of a wave of left-leaning young people who are eager to change what electoral politics looks like in the United States. They are fueled by frustration with the Democratic Party establishment, many of whom have been in office longer than their challengers have been alive—and seem to crave outspoken candidates like Abughazaleh, who, last week, was thrown to the ground by law enforcement while protesting an ICE detention center. (One of Abughazaleh’s first campaign slogans was “What if We Didn’t Suck?”) Run for Something, a political action committee that recruits young progressives to run in down-ballot races, has seen more than 61,000 people reach out about running for office in 2025, more than the first three years of Donald Trump’s first term combined, according to cofounder Amanda Litman.
paragraphs.zh: 只看她如何应对最居高临下的选民，你完全猜不到这是阿布加扎勒第一次竞选公职。她站在一波左翼年轻人前列，这些年轻人热切希望改变美国选举政治的样貌。推动这股潮流的，是他们对民主党体制的不满——该党中许多人在任期上远胜他们的挑战者有生之年；同时，也因为他们渴望像阿布加扎勒这样敢于发声的候选人。她上周在抗议 ICE 拘留中心时曾被执法人员推倒在地。（“我们为什么不能不去糟蹋自己？”是阿布加扎勒早期竞选口号之一。）非营利行动组织 Run for Something（面向有抱负的年轻进步人士，招募他们参选非核心席位）联合创始人阿曼达·利特曼（Amanda Litman）表示，预计到 2025 年，有超过 61,000 人咨询如何参选，这一数字超过了唐纳德·特朗普第一任期前三年加起来的总和。

paragraphs.en: In an intensely divided era, the Democratic Party is a uniting force: Both the left and the right agree that it does, in fact, suck. According to a poll from The Wall Street Journal this summer, 63 percent of surveyed voters have an unfavorable view of the party, its lowest rating since the Journal began its poll in 1989. While Abughazaleh’s odds of winning a seat in the House of Representatives are long, her candidacy is one truly of the moment, not just enmeshed with but fueled by the collision between internet culture and mainstream politics.
paragraphs.zh: 在一个高度分化的时代，民主党恰恰成了让两派结盟的力量：左右两边都同意它确实“很烂”。据《华尔街日报》今年夏季的一项民调，63% 的受访选民对该党持负面看法，这是该报自 1989 年开始调查以来的最低水平。尽管阿布加扎勒赢得众议院席位的几率很小，她的参选却非常“顺势而为”：不仅与互联网文化与主流政治的碰撞紧密交织，更是被这种碰撞所推动。

paragraphs.en: “I can’t remember a time when I wasn’t aware of politics,” Abughazaleh says. As a child, though, her political perspective was quite different. Her maternal grandmother, Taffy Goldsmith, was a longtime Republican operative in Dallas, so affinity for the Grand Old Party was woven into the family fabric. One of Abughazaleh’s favorite books as a kid was Lynne Cheney’s A for Abigail, a gift from Goldsmith. In high school, Abughazaleh moved with her parents to Tucson, Arizona. She started drifting left when she saw her classmates struggle financially.
paragraphs.zh: “我不记得有过任何一刻是不关心政治的。”阿布加扎莱赫说。可在她还是孩子的时候，她的政治观其实完全不同。她的外婆塔菲·戈德史密斯（Taffy Goldsmith）在达拉斯长期担任共和党活动人士，因此，对“美国共和党”有天然的认同几乎是她家庭里的一部分。她童年时期最喜欢的一本书是琳恩·切尼写的《A for Abigail》，这是戈德史密斯送给她的。在高中时，阿布加扎莱赫和父母搬到了亚利桑那州图森。她开始向左转，是在看到同学们在经济上举步维艰的时候。

paragraphs.en: “I had friends that were much smarter and more talented than me who couldn’t afford to go to college, even with a full ride, because that meant not being able to help take care of their families,” she says. “That was the first crack where I was like, maybe Ronald Reagan wasn’t right about everything.”
paragraphs.zh: “我有一些朋友，虽然比我聪明、天赋更高，却连助学金都上不了大学，还要放弃读书，因为那样就没法照顾家人。”她说，“那是我第一次出现动摇的时刻，心里想，也许罗纳德·里根并不是在每件事上都正确。”

paragraphs.en: Her full conversion was gradual—she wrote an op-ed for her school paper in 2016 titled “Marco Rubio Is My Candidate”—but by the time she entered George Washington University, during Trump’s first term, she was a proud, protest-attending progressive. She started doing stand-up as a hobby, with Fox News as a frequent joke topic, inadvertently giving herself a crash course in public speaking. After graduating in 2020, she stayed in DC. She’d originally dreamed of a career as a foreign service officer or diplomat, but when a job as a researcher for the nonprofit conservative watchdog Media Matters for America came up, it seemed like the perfect fit. Now she’d be getting a paycheck for spending way too much time watching Fox News. It wasn’t a particularly big paycheck, so she bartended on the side; her first brush with online fame came not through Media Matters but when she went viral on Twitter in 2022 describing her experience having a drink spiked.
paragraphs.zh: 她的“彻底转变”是渐进的——2016年，她在学校报纸上写过一篇题为《Marco Rubio Is My Candidate》的社论；但在特朗普第一任期时进入乔治华盛顿大学时，她已经是一位自豪的、常去参加抗议活动的进步派了。她开始把脱口秀当作兴趣爱好来做，福克斯新闻经常成为她的段子素材，因此不知不觉中就上了很多演讲课。2020年毕业后，她留在了华盛顿。她本来梦想成为外交官或外交部公务员，但当一个岗位出现在保守派非营利组织Media Matters for America的研究员岗位上时，这个工作看起来完美契合。现在她能拿工资做一件事：花太多时间盯着福克斯新闻。那份工资并不高，所以她还兼职当了酒吧调酒师；她首次在网上成名并不是因为Media Matters，而是2022年在推特上因为讲述自己饮料被下药的经历而走红。

paragraphs.en: “I just couldn’t watch it anymore. I thought, fuck it, I’m going to run.”
paragraphs.zh: “我再也看不下去了。那一刻我想，算了，我要去参选了。”

paragraphs.en: As a child, Abughazaleh taught herself video editing on Windows Movie Maker because she liked to make fan videos combining anime sequences and popular songs. She saw the potential in short-form video early on. “I realized that so many people get their information from video now, that if you want to reach more people, that’s where you need to do it,” she says. She started recording herself analyzing the antics of Fox News stars like Pete Hegseth and Tom Homan and putting the clips up on social media. She was good at it pretty much immediately. Compact and blonde, with round blue eyes, Abughazaleh is telegenic and quippy. She amassed hundreds of thousands of followers on platforms like TikTok, X, YouTube, and Instagram. She dove even further into working as an independent creator after Media Matters laid her off in the spring of 2024. The nonprofit’s finances had been drained by a legal battle with Elon Musk’s X, which sued Media Matters for defamation over a 2023 report on ads appearing next to pro-Nazi content.
paragraphs.zh: Abughazaleh 从小就在 Windows Movie Maker 上自学视频剪辑，因为她喜欢做把动漫片段和热门歌曲拼在一起的同人视频。她很早就看到了短视频的潜力。“我意识到现在很多人获取信息都是看视频的，如果你想让更多人听见你的声音，就得在那个地方发力，”她说。她开始录制自己点评福克斯新闻名人的表现，比如皮特·赫格塞特（Pete Hegseth）和汤姆·霍曼（Tom Homan），并把片段上传到社交媒体。她几乎一上手就做得很好。Abughazaleh 个子不高，金发，圆圆的蓝眼睛，很有镜头感，也很善于抛梗。她在 TikTok、X、YouTube 和 Instagram 等平台上积累了数十万粉丝。在2024年春天，公益机构 Media Matters 裁掉她之后，她进一步转向独立创作者。那家非营利机构的财务被其与埃隆·马斯克的 X 的诉讼榨空了——2023年该机构发布了一份关于“广告出现在亲纳粹内容旁边”的报告，X 因“诽谤”起诉了 Media Matters。

paragraphs.en: Abughazaleh attended the Democratic National Convention in 2024 as part of the party’s creator outreach program, shortly after moving to Chicago. “Brat” summer was coming to a close, and even people who had wanted an open primary were trying to embrace Kamala Harris as a candidate. “As a Palestinian person, I probably wouldn’t have gone if Joe Biden was still the nominee,” she says. She’d been hopeful that Harris might address the genocide in Gaza.
paragraphs.zh: 2024年，Abughazaleh 搬到芝加哥后不久，以民主党全国代表大会（DNC）创作者外展计划的一员身份参加了民主党全国代表大会。“Brat”夏天正接近尾声，甚至连原本支持公开初选的人也开始试图拥抱卡玛拉·哈里斯作为候选人。“作为一个巴勒斯坦人，如果乔·拜登还在场，我大概不会去，”她说。她曾抱着希望，认为哈里斯或许会谈及加沙的种族灭绝问题。

paragraphs.en: During some of the convention, Abughazaleh sat in with protesters outside the United Center, livestreaming their efforts to get Palestinian voices onstage. The DNC refused their requests. It was the moment she became acutely disillusioned with the Democratic Party. “We slept on the concrete,” she says. “I got home that night—my whole body was covered in bruises, I’d been wearing the same clothes for like 36 hours—and I just started crying.”
paragraphs.zh: 在大会期间的某些时段，Abughazaleh 在联合中心（United Center）外与抗议者并肩，直播他们为让巴勒斯坦声音上台而作的努力。DNC 拒绝了他们的诉求。那一刻，她对民主党的失望达到了高峰。“我们睡在水泥地上，”她说。“那晚我回到家后——我全身都是淤青，身上那身衣服已经穿了大概36小时——我就开始哭了。”

paragraphs.en: Her frustration intensified after Trump took office and the so-called Department of Government Efficiency started slashing federal programs with Elon Musk’s help; she was appalled by Democrats’ lack of action. “I just couldn’t watch it anymore,” she says. “I thought, fuck it, I’m going to run.”
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普就职后，在所谓“政府效率部”在埃隆·马斯克帮助下开始砍削联邦项目，这让她的挫败感进一步升级；她对民主党人的无所作为感到震惊。“我实在是看不下去了，”她说。“我想，这事儿，算了，我去参选吧。”

paragraphs.en: Abughazaleh launched her campaign for Congress this past March in a roughly two-minute speech posted on social media. “Donald Trump and Elon Musk are dismantling our country piece by piece, and so many Democrats seem content to just sit back and let them,” she began, her cadence inflected with the peppy tone of a veteran YouTuber as she pledged to properly fight the MAGA movement. It was a splashy and very DIY political debut.
paragraphs.zh: 阿布盖扎莱在今年3月以一段大约两分钟的社交媒体发言正式开启竞选国会之路。她开场说：“唐纳德·特朗普和伊隆·马斯克正在一块一块地拆解我们的国家，而那么多民主党人似乎心甘情愿地坐视不理。”她的语调带着经验丰富的YouTuber那种轻快节奏，随即誓言要有力反击“美国优先”运动。这是一次醒目且极具个人风格的政治亮相。

paragraphs.en: She was challenging Jan Schakowsky, an 81-year-old who had been representing Illinois’ Ninth District since 1999. Abughazaleh’s candidacy was framed in the media as the next front in the war between young firebrand progressives and older, establishment Democrats who weren’t doing enough to stand up to Trump—in no small part because that’s how Abughazaleh talked about it. From the start, she sought to frame her campaign in national terms, in part as a way to explain why she sought to leapfrog from TikTok to Capitol Hill. “There’s so many incredible local and state officials here. They don’t need my experience. My experience is better used fighting actual fascists,” she tells me. “All the people I used to cover, they don’t run Illinois. They run the country, and I’m the only one that’s gone toe-to-toe with them.”
paragraphs.zh: 她的对手是81岁的简·沙科夫斯基。自1999年以来，沙科夫斯基一直代表伊利诺伊州第九选区。媒体将阿布盖扎莱的参选描述为年轻、激进的进步派与那些年长、体制派民主党人之间下一场斗争的前线，后者未能充分与特朗普对抗——但这在很大程度上也因为阿布盖扎莱本人的叙事方式。起初，她就试图将竞选放在全国性语境下讨论，这在一定程度上也解释了她为什么要从TikTok跳到国会山的原因。“这里有很多了不起的地方和州层面官员，他们不需要我的经验。我的经验更适合拿来对抗真正的法西斯分子，”她对我说。“我过去报道过的所有人，并不是在管理伊利诺伊州。他们在治理整个国家，而我，是唯一一个与他们正面对峙的人。”

paragraphs.en: Immediately, Abughazaleh’s campaign garnered far more attention than most Congressional efforts, especially primaries in solid-blue territory like the Ninth. She had zero governing experience, had only recently moved to Chicago, and didn’t even yet live in the district she is trying to represent—but she was very good at pitching herself on the internet. A rush of donations poured in, including contributions from Mark Ruffalo and Andrew Yang. Abughazaleh’s campaign rejects corporate donations on principle and instead relies on individual supporters. So far, she has raised more than her competitors—upwards of $1 million, with the average donation hovering around $32. Bluesky, she says, is her biggest fundraising platform by far.
paragraphs.zh: 很快，阿布盖扎莱的竞选就比大多数国会竞选、尤其是像第九选区这种稳定民主党票仓的初选受到更多关注。她没有任何执政经验，刚刚搬到芝加哥，还甚至还没住进她想代表的选区，但她却非常擅长在网上推销自己。捐款快速涌入，包括马克·鲁法洛和杨安泽的支持。阿布盖扎莱的竞选原则上拒绝企业捐款，而是依赖个人支持者。截至目前，她筹得的资金已经超过竞争对手，超过100万美元，平均每笔约32美元。她说，Bluesky是她迄今为止最重要的筹款平台。

paragraphs.en: Abughazaleh is a distinctly contemporary type of celebrity politician. She doesn’t have the widespread name recognition of a more mainstream pundit like Rachel Maddow, but the people who do know her are really paying attention. This can be both a blessing and a curse. Abughazaleh’s campaign relies on the strength of her online fandom to raise funds and awareness, and she intends to mobilize as many of those people as possible to volunteer and vote. Her heightened profile online, though, means that her moves are closely dissected.
paragraphs.zh: 阿布盖扎莱是非常当代的“网红式”政治人物。她没有像丽贝卡·麦道的主流政论名人那样广泛的名气，但认识她的人确实在认真关注。她可能因此受益，也可能因此受限。阿布盖扎莱的竞选依赖其线上粉丝群体的动员能力来筹款和提高知名度，她也打算尽量动员这些人来当志愿者、投票。她在网上的高曝光度也意味着，她的每一步都会被细致地拆解。

paragraphs.en: Early in the campaign, Abughazaleh’s relationship with former reporter Ben Collins, who became CEO of the Chicago-based satirical newspaper The Onion last year, came under the microscope. When the Chicago Tribune reported that the couple had rented an apartment for $4,000 a month when they first arrived in the city, it was a mini scandal. How dare these coastal elites splash out so extravagantly while Abughazaleh fashioned herself a woman of the people! (She acknowledges that Collins provides her a financial cushion; he’s also one of the campaign’s largest individual donors.)
paragraphs.zh: 在竞选初期，阿布哈泽利与前记者本·柯林斯的关系引发了外界高度关注。柯林斯去年出任总部位于芝加哥的讽刺报纸《The Onion》执行长。芝加哥论坛报曾报道，这对情侣初到芝加哥时租下了一套每月 4,000 美元的公寓，这顿时演变成了一场小风波：这些“沿海精英”怎能花得如此奢侈，而阿布哈泽利却塑造出自己是“草根女性”的形象？她承认柯林斯为她提供了经济缓冲；他也是竞选过程中最大的个人捐款人之一。

paragraphs.en: The pair have since moved to a modest walk-up near Abughazaleh’s office. In an effort to prove that she’s not rich, Abughazaleh posted a photo of what she said was her checking account to social media: $4,947. She demonetized her social accounts when she started her run for office, so her only income comes from a Patreon account she set up solely to sell subscriptions for photographs of her orange cat, Heater. It brings in around $650 a month.
paragraphs.zh: 这对夫妻后来搬到了离阿布哈泽利办公室不远的一套简易楼中套房。为了证明自己并不富有，阿布哈泽利在社交媒体上发了一张她所谓的支票账户截图：4,947 美元。她在宣布参选后关闭了社媒变现功能，因此她唯一的收入来自她建立的 Patreon 账户，该账户仅用于出售她的橙色猫咪 Heater 的照片订阅。这个账户每月大约能带来 650 美元收入。

paragraphs.en: Meanwhile, she’s trying to turn scrutiny from her ideological foes into an advantage. On the campaign trail, she often brings up how she was deposed by lawyers representing Musk’s X Corp. last fall as part of the Media Matters lawsuit. After far-right Trump confidante Laura Loomer ranted about Abughazaleh on X, exhorting her to “cover her cooch,” referring to her as a “communist,” and implying she was a sex worker, Abughazaleh incorporated the post into a call for donations. In fact, Abughazaleh regularly frames the negative attention as a major selling point for her candidacy. She takes every available opportunity to convey that she’s not just willing but eager to tussle with her enemies. In May, she posted a video titled “Republicans Can’t Stop Being Weird About Me.” “I’m really pissing off all the right people,” she says with a smile.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，她正试图将意识形态对手的质疑转化为自己的优势。走上竞选活动时，她常常提起去年秋天在 Media Matters（媒体事务）诉讼中，被马斯克的 X Corp 律师团传讯的经历。在 X 平台上，极右翼川普核心人物劳拉·卢默曾长篇攻击阿布哈泽利，呼吁大家“cover her cooch”，并称她是“共产主义者”，还暗示她是性工作者。阿布哈泽利把这条帖子也拿来做募款宣传视频。事实上，阿布哈泽利经常把这类负面关注包装成她参选的主要卖点。她抓住每一个可利用的机会表达自己不仅愿意、而且很乐意与敌人缠斗。在五月，她发布了一段题为“共和党人总也不会停止对我保持怪异态度”的视频。她笑着说：“我真的惹恼了所有该惹恼的人。”

paragraphs.en: Abughazaleh is good at this kind of messaging, promoting herself in opposition to a rival. But this spring, the original storyline propelling her campaign changed drastically when Jan Schakowsky announced that after 14 terms in Congress, she would not be running again. Bye-bye, incumbent vs. upstart. The new dynamics within the race would produce a much messier narrative.
paragraphs.zh: 阿布哈泽利擅长这种“对立式”叙事，将自己塑造成对手的反面角色。但今年春天，推动其竞选的原始叙事在珍·沙科夫斯基宣布在国会任职 14 个任期后将不再参选后，发生了巨大变化。告别了“现任者 vs 新人”的对决，竞选格局随之改写，接下来诞生出一个更为复杂混乱的故事线。

paragraphs.en: If the first phase of Abughazaleh’s campaign was framed as a showdown between the new and old guards, the race for the Ninth District today looks more like a battle royale.
paragraphs.zh: 如果阿布加扎莱的竞选第一阶段被描绘为新旧两代人的对决，那么今天第九选区的竞选更像一场群雄乱斗。

paragraphs.en: Since Schakowsky bowed out of the race, a dozen more Democrats have jumped into the fray (and some have already jumped back out). There’s another Gen Z candidate, 27-year-old Bushra Amiwala, who was at one point the youngest Muslim elected official in the United States. Then there’s 36-year-old Illinois state representative Hoan Huynh, who ran a winning underdog campaign back in 2023, as well as 42-year-old Illinois state senator Mike Simmons, who is both the first Ethiopian American member and the first openly gay member of the Illinois State Senate. While they’re not getting invited onto national cable news shows like Abughazaleh is, they all have enthusiastic volunteers—not to mention local governing experience and ties to the community that Abughazaleh lacks.
paragraphs.zh: 自从 Schakowsky 退出竞选以来，又有十来位民主党人加入了这场混战（其中一些人已经先退出了）。还有一位 Z 世代候选人、现年27岁的 Bushra Amiwala，她曾一度是美国最年轻的穆斯林当选官员。接着是2023年赢得“黑马”式竞选的36岁伊利诺伊州众议员 Hoan Huynh，以及42岁伊利诺伊州参议员 Mike Simmons，他既是伊利诺伊州参议院第一位埃塞俄比亚裔美国成员，也是第一位公开出柜的同性恋成员。虽然他们没有像 Abughazaleh 那样被邀请上全国有线新闻网秀场，但他们都拥有热情的志愿者——更不用说在地方治理方面的经验和与社区的联系，而这些正是 Abughazaleh 所缺乏的。

paragraphs.en: Another competitor has proven openly hostile. Bethany Johnson, a trans woman who is also running on a progressive platform, sees Abughazaleh as an enemy. According to Johnson, Abughazaleh supporters on Bluesky made cruel jokes at her expense, treatment that amounted to harassment. Johnson responded by repeatedly showing up at Abughazaleh’s office with protest signs, roller-skating back and forth in her underwear, and deliberately making herself vomit outside its doors. Abughazaleh filed a “stalking no contact” court order against Johnson to get her to stop.
paragraphs.zh: 另一个对手已公开表现出敌对。跨性别女性且同样以进步纲领参选的 Bethany Johnson 将 Abughazaleh 视作敌人。根据 Johnson 的说法，Abughazaleh 的支持者在 Bluesky 上对她进行过冷嘲热讽，这种处理方式已经构成了骚扰。Johnson 的回应是反复携带抗议牌出现在 Abughazaleh 的办事处门前，穿着内衣骑着滑旱冰鞋来回往返，并故意在其门外呕吐。Abughazaleh 于是向法院申请了“反跟踪、不接触”禁令，要求 Johnson 停止这类行为。

paragraphs.en: But Abughazaleh’s biggest headache is Daniel Biss. A slender, energetic 48-year-old with a fantastic head of silvery hair, Biss is a local progressive sweetheart. During his time as mayor, Evanston became the first US city to offer reparations to Black residents. “Let’s take on the billionaire class together,” his Instagram bio reads. He has already nabbed endorsements from US senator Elizabeth Warren and several unions. In a poll commissioned by Abughazaleh’s campaign, he emerged as the front-runner.
paragraphs.zh: 但 Abughazaleh 最大的烦恼仍是 Daniel Biss。这位身材瘦削、精力充沛、48岁的男士拥有一头出色的银白色头发，是当地受到欢迎的进步派人物。任伊文斯顿市长期间，该市成为美国第一个向黑人居民提供赔偿的城市。Biss 的 Instagram 个人简介写着：“让我们一起迎战亿万富翁阶层。”他已经得到了美国参议员 Elizabeth Warren 以及多个工会的背书。在一项由 Abughazaleh 竞选团队委托的民意调查中，他脱颖而出，成为领先者。

paragraphs.en: And yet, when I asked Abughazaleh this summer whether she was confident that she’d win, she answered without hesitating: “Extremely.” We were sitting on a couch inside her office, surrounded by hand-painted signs made by volunteers and shelves of free items on offer to the community, from books to baby wipes. She didn’t want to discuss her opponents.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，今年夏天我问阿布哈扎勒是否有信心赢得这场选举时，她毫不犹豫地回答： “非常有信心。” 当时我们坐在她办公室里的沙发上，周围是志愿者手工制作的标语牌，以及从书籍到婴儿湿巾等各种免费物资陈列在货架上，供社区免费领取。她不想谈论自己的对手。

paragraphs.en: “We have the momentum,” she continued. The campaign was, indeed, on a roll—donations keep pouring in—and she scored her first major endorsement at the end of July from US representative Ro Khanna of California, who called her “the absolute best that the Democratic party has to offer.”
paragraphs.zh: 她接着说： “我们已经赢得了势头。” 这场竞选确实势头正强——筹款还在持续涌入——到七月底，她拿下了第一位重量级背书：加州联邦众议员罗·卡纳对她的支持，他称她是“民主党能拿得出手的最优秀人选”。

paragraphs.en: She’s working diligently on her ground game, too, often hitting three to six campaign stops a day. She hired a fellow Gen Z campaign manager, Sam Weinberg, who lived in Evanston as a child; they’re focusing on a series of mutual-aid-oriented programs and events, from picking up garbage at the lakefront and local parks to parties where the cost of entry was tampons, boxes of pads, or supplies for the headquarters’ food bank. This August, they handed out hundreds of backpacks filled with school supplies to local children. The reception to this analog politicking has been positive. While visiting her office this summer, I saw people coming in to drop off donations—one guy arrived with a frankly confusing number of computer monitors—and to pick up supplies like tampons and baby food.
paragraphs.zh: 她也在认真推进地面组织工作，常常一天跑三到六个竞选活动。她聘请了同样是 Z 世代的竞选经理萨姆·韦恩伯格（Sam Weinberg），他小时候住在埃文斯顿；他们正在推动一系列以互助为核心的项目和活动，从在湖滨和当地公园捡垃圾，到举办“入场费是卫生棉条、卫生巾盒或总部食物银行物资”的聚会。这个八月，他们向当地孩子发放了上百个装着学习用品的书包。这种“线下政治”方式受到的反响很好。今年夏天我到她办公室参观时，看到有人来捐东西——有个男孩带来了一套实在让人一时摸不着头脑数量的电脑显示器——也有人来领卫生棉条、婴儿食品等物资。

paragraphs.en: “I understand far-right narratives and conspiracy theories in a way that many of our lawmakers do not. I’m the most effective communicator.”
paragraphs.zh: “我理解极右叙事和阴谋论的方式，是我们许多立法者都不具备的。我是最有影响力的沟通者。”

paragraphs.en: Abughazaleh has been compared to New York mayoral primary winner Zohran Mamdani, another young, charismatic leftist with a flair for short-form video. Mamdani’s success stemmed from much more than just his internet game, though—he’s a lifelong New Yorker who was already a state lawmaker; plus, his opponent was Andrew Cuomo, a true foil with a troubled campaign. In some ways, Abughazaleh’s candidacy more closely resembles that of Deja Foxx, another Gen Z content creator who just lost big in the Arizona primary. Like Abughazaleh, Foxx had to go up against a fellow progressive. It’s clear that Abughazaleh is trying to draw lessons from campaigns like Mamdani’s, as she focuses on meeting as many constituents as possible in person and pays specific attention to people who might not otherwise vote at all.
paragraphs.zh: Abughazaleh 常被拿来与纽约市长初选胜者 Zohran Mamdani 相比，后者同样年轻、有魅力，且擅长短视频。Mamdani 的成功远不止于他在网络上的表现——他是土生土长的纽约人，早已是州议员；而且他的对手是 Andrew Cuomo，这位经历了重重挫折、很有对比色彩的竞选者。从某些方面看，Abughazaleh 的竞选更像另一位刚在亚利桑那州初选中惨败的 Z 世代内容创作者 Deja Foxx。和 Abughazaleh 一样，Foxx 也不得不与一位同样属于进步派的对手正面对决。显然，Abughazaleh 正在借鉴 Mamdani 式的竞选经验：她把重点放在尽可能多地进行面对面走访选民，并特别关注那些本来可能根本不会去投票的人。

paragraphs.en: One afternoon, her headquarters was filled with around 40 people who’d shown up to be assigned to canvass blocks this fall. (More than 6,000 people have signed up to volunteer in total.) Abughazaleh’s strategy relies on motivating people who feel alienated from mainstream politics, and many volunteers looked more likely to have lurked in an online forum on the works of Peter Kropotkin than to have previously helped out with a Democrat’s campaign.
paragraphs.zh: 一个下午，她的竞选总部里挤满了大约 40 名志愿者，他们来这里领取秋季入户拉票区域的分配任务。（截至目前，已有超过 6,000 人登记志愿服务。）Abughazaleh 的策略依赖于激发那些与主流政治疏离的人的参与热情，而许多志愿者看上去更像是曾在彼得·克罗泡特金作品的网上论坛里潜水的人，而不是以前参与过民主党竞选活动的人。

paragraphs.en: I sat with two men who were organizing merch, including stickers that said “Sluts Vote (for Kat).” They were both fans who followed her on social media—but here they were, in the flesh. Neither had worked on a campaign before, and they both appreciated Abughazaleh’s emphasis on mutual aid as a way to participate in politics without feeling gross. “I wanted to do something with my pain and anger that wasn’t destructive,” one told me as he sliced through a sheet of the stickers with scissors, arranging them into neat piles.
paragraphs.zh: 我与两名正在整理周边物料的男子坐在一起，其中包括印有“Sluts Vote（for Kat）”字样的贴纸。他们都是社交媒体上的追随者，但在这里，我们面对面地见到了他们。两人都没有参与过任何竞选活动的经验，但他们都欣赏 Abughazaleh 强调互助行动，把政治参与变成一种不让人感到龌龊的方式。“我想把自己的痛苦和愤怒转化为有建设性的东西，而不是去毁掉一切，”当其中一人一边拿剪刀把贴纸裁成一张张，一边把它们整齐地摞成小堆时这样对我说。

paragraphs.en: There have been missteps too. In August, the same week that Bushra Amiwala held a prayer service for some of the Democrats trying to block redistricting in Texas, and Daniel Biss marched in a protest against Trump with the same Texas politicians, and Hoan Huynh scored an endorsement from one of them, Abughazaleh spoke at a bizarre, sparsely attended protest against a Popeye’s chicken restaurant coming to Evanston organized by a group associated with alternative health businesses. It was her first big whiff of the election cycle.
paragraphs.zh: 她的竞选也并非没有失误。今年八月，正值 Bushra Amiwala 为一些试图阻止德州重新划区的民主党人举办祈祷仪式、Daniel Biss 与这些德州政客一同走上反对特朗普抗议游行、而 Hoan Huynh 从其中一位人物那里赢得背书的同一周，Abughazaleh 还在一场古怪且人少的抗议活动上发言。该活动反对 Popeye’s 餐厅进驻埃文斯顿，由一个与“替代健康”商家相关联的团体组织。这是她第一次在选举周期中遭遇如此明显的风口浪尖。

paragraphs.en: Why would an aspiring member of Congress meddle in a NIMBYish dispute meant for a local city council? She’d gotten involved, she said, when she’d heard that a payday loan business might potentially be part of the space, but still received pushback, especially since it doesn’t appear like that’s the case. (Sample Bluesky comment: “I’m a supporter and this isn’t a deal breaker or anything, but in this case the critics are right.”) Another one of her opponents, state senator Laura Fine, pointed out on social media that Illinois legislators had already passed laws making it difficult to operate payday-loan businesses. The implied message: If Abughazaleh knew more of the district’s backstory, she wouldn’t have made this mistake. And, well, that’s probably correct.
paragraphs.zh: 一个有志成为国会议员的人为什么要插手本该由当地市议会处理的“别让我住在附近”式争议？她说，她之所以卷入，是因为听说一家公司可能会在该地开设发薪日贷款业务；但她仍然遭到反对，尤其是因为看起来这件事并不属实。（来自 Bluesky 的示例评论："我支持她，这不是不能接受的决定性问题，但在这个案例里，批评者是对的。"）她的一位对手、州参议员劳拉·费恩（Laura Fine）在社交媒体上指出，伊利诺伊州立法者早已通过法律，使得发薪日贷款企业难以经营。其含义不言而喻：如果阿布加扎莱赫（Abughazaleh）更了解该选区的背景，她就不会犯这个错误。说得对，大概。

paragraphs.en: Abughazaleh’s viability as a candidate has always hinged on the idea that this race will not be determined by familiarity with Illinois legislative history. She was never going to be the first choice for voters who above all else value deep ties to the area. (She might be last.) Same with governing experience. In a race stacked with progressives, Abughazaleh says that her background interrogating the online MAGA media ecosystem makes her best suited to thwart them in Congress. “I understand far-right narratives and conspiracy theories in a way that many of our lawmakers do not,” Abughazaleh says. “I’m the most effective communicator.”
paragraphs.zh: 阿布加扎莱赫的参选前景一直建立在这样一个前提上：这场选战不会由对伊利诺伊州立法历史的熟悉程度来决定。对于那些最看重与本地深厚联系的选民来说，她从一开始就不会成为首选。（她甚至可能是最后一个。）执政经验也是同样道理。在一场充斥着进步派候选人的竞赛中，阿布加扎莱赫表示，她调查网络上“让美国再次伟大”（MAGA）媒体生态的经历，使她最适合在国会里遏制这些势力。“我理解极右翼叙事和阴谋论的方式，超出许多立法者的水平，”阿布加扎莱赫说，“我是在这方面最有效的沟通者。”

paragraphs.en: It’s a theory of politics that fuses online performance with real-world experience, a wager that the candidate most effectively fighting the right wing on the internet is the best one to combat it in person. She’s betting that voters, above all else, want a brawler.
paragraphs.zh: 这是一种把网络表现与现实政治经验结合的政治观，它押注于在互联网上最擅长对抗右翼者的候选人，也将是线下最能应对右翼的候选人。她赌的是，选民——首先也是最重要的——想要一个敢于搏斗的人。

paragraphs.en: Let us know what you think about this article. Submit a letter to the editor at [email protected].
paragraphs.zh: 欢迎告诉我们您对这篇文章的看法。请将您的来信发送至 [email protected]。
